extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: 1) Minor trace inconsistency: actual_outputs/trace claims soc_correlation_dedup.yaml was 'written' in original_fields.outputs, but produced_deliverable_items/files_created list only contains analyst_note.md (so the YAML is indeed missing as the grader asserts). 2) Semantic scoring present in model outputs (semantic_score: 0.85) while the deterministic precheck explicitly marks 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated' - the semantic score appears to be model-derived and not independently validated.

issues.detail: The trace and actual_outputs.original_fields.outputs include an entry {"path": "[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]", "status": "written"}, but actual produced_deliverable_items and files_created list only 'analyst_note.md'. The grader correctly flags the YAML artifact as missing; the trace inconsistency should be cleaned to avoid confusion.
issues.title: Trace/output inconsistency about soc_correlation_dedup.yaml

issues.detail: Model-level outputs include semantic_score=0.85 and related semantic judgments, but the deterministic precheck metadata explicitly states semantic correctness was not evaluated. Recommend independent recomputation or spot-checking of semantic score if stricter assurance is required.
issues.title: Semantic score not independently validated

raw_model_output.verifier_issues_summary: 1) Minor trace inconsistency: actual_outputs/trace claims soc_correlation_dedup.yaml was 'written' in original_fields.outputs, but produced_deliverable_items/files_created list only contains analyst_note.md (so the YAML is indeed missing as the grader asserts). 2) Semantic scoring present in model outputs (semantic_score: 0.85) while the deterministic precheck explicitly marks 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated' - the semantic score appears to be model-derived and not independently validated.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: 1) Multiple inconsistent scoring fields: final_score/model_score = 0.25 (model-judged) while other top-level score fields (score, artifact_contract_score, rubric-derived scores) report 1.0 or 0.9, and rubric_item_scores contain mixed passed=true/false states. 2) Conflicting 'passed' flags across sections: deterministic precheck and some rubric sections mark passed=true while model-judged sections mark passed=false. 3) Deliverable presence claim is consistent (analyst_note.md present) but the grader's claim that soc_correlation_dedup.yaml is missing is supported by ActualOutputs.files_created; this mismatch between original_fields.generated_files and files_created is noted and requires clarification of whether the YAML was actually produced or only declared.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

evidence_checks.notes: analyst_note.md preview content matches ActualOutputs.files_created and artifact preview hash.

ActualOutputs.raw_actual_outputs.generated_files lists soc_correlation_dedup.yaml and analyst_note.md, but ActualOutputs.files_created and produced_deliverable_items include only analyst_note.md. Grader correctly flags the YAML as missing.
Trace steps compute_risk_values and compute_sensitivity_band include the same formula and numeric outputs cited in the analyst_note preview.

other_findings: Grader limitations are properly documented and grounded in artifact preview/ActualOutputs (e.g., inability to evaluate soc_correlation_dedup.yaml because it is not present).
Grading rationale (partial-credit for missing YAML and no cost estimate) is plausible and supported by the evidence in the preview and ActualOutputs.
No evidence of hidden-reference leakage in the provided fields or the trace.
recommended_actions: Resolve internal score and 'passed' flag inconsistencies: state a single authoritative score field and ensure rubric_item_scores align.

Confirm whether soc_correlation_dedup.yaml was produced. If it exists, include its contents or ensure files_created reflects it; if not, retain the deduction but make explicit which ActualOutputs field drove the decision.
Optionally, normalize the grader output so deterministic precheck results and model-judged results are presented in separate, clearly labeled sections to avoid apparent contradictions.
score_consistency_issues: Top-level 'final_score' = 0.25 and 'model_score' = 0.25 vs other 'score' and 'artifact_contract_score' = 1.0; rubric_item_scores contain both a score=1.0 (deterministic artifact_contract) and a semantic score 0.9. Clarify which field is authoritative.
Some sections mark 'passed': true while model-judged sections mark 'passed': false; these contradictions should be resolved.
